issues_found_and_fixed: Arabic RTL mode created a 9,999px horizontal document overflow because the skip link was hidden with left:-9999px.
The closed RTL mobile menu was positioned off the wrong edge and was not contained by its overlay.
Arabic headings inherited Latin proportions, letter spacing and line-height, producing oversized and cramped typography.
Arabic WE CAN SVG text used forced Latin textLength values.
Compact build and connected descriptions were truncated too aggressively.
Connected-scene camera interpolation clipped models during service transitions on every tested device class.
Connected initialization competed with the page startup instead of using near-viewport/idle preloading.
corrections: Replaced offscreen skip-link positioning with an accessible transform-based focus reveal.
Corrected RTL mobile-menu anchoring, transform direction and overflow containment.
Added Arabic-specific responsive typography, Noto Arabic fallbacks, balanced line-height, and zero Arabic tracking.
Removed forced textLength/lengthAdjust attributes from Arabic WE CAN SVG text.
Allowed complete compact build/connected descriptions without line-clamp clipping.
Replaced connected transition interpolation with a frame-safe camera retreat/cut while keeping exact scroll mapping.
Moved connected preload to intersection/idle scheduling to reduce initial loading contention.
Removed legacy connected versions and obsolete audit files from the deliverable.
validation_note: Three.js geometry, responsive camera calculations and DOM layouts were executed in Chromium with a structural renderer because this environment does not expose a real WebGL/GPU context. JavaScript runtime, scene construction, camera projection, RTL layout and asset loading were exercised.
